@@ -585,7 +585,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-s797","title":"Tutorial Path: Quick Start (7 Steps)","description":"# Purpose Implement the Quick Start tutorial path — 7 steps that take a user from "What is this?" to "I have a research artifact for my own question" in approximately 30 minutes. This is the **primary onboarding funnel**. Most users will take this path. It must be polished, failure-tolerant, and genuinely useful. --- # Core Constraint **No complex infrastructure required.** This path works with: - A terminal - Git - Bun - Any AI chat interface (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini — even free tiers) No Agent Mail. No ntm. No subscriptions beyond basic access. --- # Step-by-Step Breakdown ## Step 1: What Is This? (~3 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/1/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Explain BrennerBot in 60 seconds - Present the Two Axioms prominently - Show a completed artifact (screenshot or inline example) - Set expectations for the path **Key Content**: \`\`\`markdown # What Is BrennerBot? Sydney Brenner (1927-2019) was one of the most successful experimental biologists in history. But his **method** is more valuable than any single discovery. BrennerBot operationalizes that method for AI-assisted research. ## The Two Axioms 1. **Reality has a generative grammar** — the world is produced by causal machinery with discoverable rules. 2. **To understand is to reconstruct** — you haven't explained something until you can build it from primitives. ## What You'll Produce By the end of this tutorial, you'll have a **research artifact** for your own scientific question containing: - 2-5 competing hypotheses (including a "third alternative") - Discriminative tests that separate the hypotheses - An explicit assumption ledger - Adversarial critiques of your framing Let's start. \`\`\` --- ## Step 2: Prerequisites (~2 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/2/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - OS-specific instructions (tabs for Mac, Windows, Linux) - Interactive checklist (user confirms each prereq) - Skip affordance for experienced users **Prerequisites**: 1. Terminal access 2. Git installed 3. Bun installed **Code Blocks**: \`\`\`bash # Check git git --version # Check bun (or install) bun --version || curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash # Verify bun --version \`\`\` **Troubleshooting**: - "bun: command not found" → Add to PATH, restart terminal - Windows: Use WSL2 (link to instructions) - Permission errors: sudo or user install --- ## Step 3: Clone & Install (~5 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/3/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Get the repo locally - Install dependencies - Verify installation with doctor command **Commands**: \`\`\`bash # Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/brenner_bot.git cd brenner_bot # Install dependencies bun install # Verify everything works ./brenner doctor \`\`\` **Expected Output**: \`\`\` BrennerBot Doctor Report ======================== ✓ Bun runtime: 1.x.x ✓ Dependencies installed ✓ Corpus files present ✓ CLI executable ⚠ Agent Mail: not running (optional for Quick Start) \`\`\` **Checkpoint**: "You've cloned BrennerBot and verified your installation!" --- ## Step 4: Search the Corpus (~5 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/4/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Teach corpus search - Show how results include §n anchors - User searches for their own topic **Interactive Example**: \`\`\`bash # Search for a topic ./brenner corpus search "pattern formation" # Try your own domain ./brenner corpus search "YOUR_TOPIC_HERE" \`\`\` **Explanation**: - Results show transcript segments with §n references - These are stable anchors for citation - High-quality matches ground your research in Brenner's thinking **User Exercise**: "Search for something related to YOUR research domain. Note 3-5 §n references that seem relevant." --- ## Step 5: Build an Excerpt (~5 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/5/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Compose relevant transcript sections into an excerpt - Explain why excerpts matter (grounding, citation) - Save excerpt for use in next step **Commands**: \`\`\`bash # Build excerpt from specific sections ./brenner excerpt build --sections 58,78,161 > my_excerpt.md # Or use search results to identify sections ./brenner corpus search "your topic" --format sections ./brenner excerpt build --sections THOSE_SECTIONS > my_excerpt.md \`\`\` **Excerpt Template**: \`\`\`markdown # Research Excerpt ## Relevant Brenner Segments ### §58: Reduction to one dimension "The best thing in science is to work out of phase..." ### §78: The importance of choice "You have to choose the right problem..." [Your selected segments here] \`\`\` --- ## Step 6: Your First Session (~8 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/6/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Compose a kickoff prompt using CLI - Run in "local mode" (no Agent Mail) - User copies output to their AI chat interface **The Big Moment**: This is where users actually DO Brenner-style research. **Commands**: \`\`\`bash # Compose a kickoff prompt (local mode) ./brenner prompt compose \\ --excerpt-file my_excerpt.md \\ --question "How do cells determine their position in a developing embryo?" \\ --role unified \\ --output-file my_prompt.md # View the composed prompt cat my_prompt.md \`\`\` **User Action**: 1. Copy the contents of my_prompt.md 2. Paste into Claude, ChatGPT, or Gemini 3. Run the conversation 4. Save the AI's response **Pro Tip**: "The prompt includes specific instructions for the AI to produce structured output. Don't modify these instructions — they encode the Brenner methodology." --- ## Step 7: Understand the Output (~5 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/7/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Walk through each artifact section - Explain why each matters - Connect back to Brenner's method **Artifact Sections Explained**: **Hypothesis Slate**: - 2-5 competing explanations - Must include "third alternative" (both leading theories could be wrong) - Each has mechanism + anchors **Discriminative Tests**: - Tests that SEPARATE hypotheses, not just confirm one - Potency checks ensure negative results mean something - Object transposition: choose the system that makes the test cheap **Assumption Ledger**: - Explicit load-bearing beliefs - Scale/physics constraints - Everything the hypotheses depend on **Adversarial Critique**: - Attacks on the framing itself - "Real third alternative" check - Forces you to think harder **Checkpoint**: "You've applied the Brenner method to your own research question!" **Next Steps**: - Refine your artifact based on the critique section - Try the Agent-Assisted path for higher leverage - Explore the Multi-Agent Cockpit for serious research programs --- # Testing Requirements - E2E: complete path from step 1 to step 7 - Test on Mac, Windows (WSL), Linux - Test with slow network (large clone) - Test with pre-existing brenner_bot clone (idempotent) --- # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 7 steps implemented with full content - [ ] Code blocks have copy functionality - [ ] Troubleshooting section for each step - [ ] Checkpoints at steps 3 and 7 - [ ] Mobile-responsive design - [ ] Time estimates accurate (tested) - [ ] Works with only free AI tiers","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:50:48.557736851-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:29:42.720638319-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T15:29:42.720638319-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-s797","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:54:04.466878203-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-s8x","title":"Optimize mobile transcript performance with progressive loading","description":"## Context Mobile scroll was fixed (commit 8036000) by rendering all sections in document flow instead of using virtualization. This works but has performance implications. ## Current State - Lighthouse mobile score: 80-81 - FCP: 2.7s (target: <1.5s) - LCP: 4.2s (target: <2.5s) - Script evaluation: ~7.3 seconds for 234 sections ## Implementation (BLOCKED) The implementation was completed multiple times but keeps getting reverted by an external file watcher or linter process. The code changes are: ### 1. Add constants and state (lines 571-584) \`\`\`typescript const MOBILE_INITIAL_SECTIONS = 8; const MOBILE_LOAD_INCREMENT = 8; // In component: const [mobileSectionsLoaded, setMobileSectionsLoaded] = useState(MOBILE_INITIAL_SECTIONS); const loadMoreSentinelRef = useRef(null); \`\`\` ### 2. Add IntersectionObserver effect (after virtualizer) \`\`\`typescript useEffect(() => { if (typeof window === "undefined" || window.innerWidth >= 1024) return; const sentinel = loadMoreSentinelRef.current; if (!sentinel) return; const observer = new IntersectionObserver( (entries) => { if (entries[0]?.isIntersecting && mobileSectionsLoaded < data.sections.length) { setMobileSectionsLoaded((n) => Math.min(n + MOBILE_LOAD_INCREMENT, data.sections.length)); } }, { rootMargin: "300px" } ); observer.observe(sentinel); return () => observer.disconnect(); }, [mobileSectionsLoaded, data.sections.length]); \`\`\` ### 3. Update mobile rendering \`\`\`typescript {data.sections.slice(0, mobileSectionsLoaded).map(...)} {mobileSectionsLoaded < data.sections.length && (
Loading spinner...
)} \`\`\` ### 4. Update scrollToSection Handle loading target section before scrolling. ## BLOCKER File changes to TranscriptViewer.tsx are automatically reverted by some process. Need to identify and disable the watcher/hook before this can be implemented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T02:24:48.653428197-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:32:57.850333349-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T02:29:59.563357022-05:00","close_reason":"Progressive loading implemented: Initial 8 sections load on mobile, IntersectionObserver with 300px rootMargin loads 8 more as user scrolls, scrollToSection loads target section before scrolling. Build passes."} {"id":"brenner_bot-se2r","title":"TASK: Define Hypothesis Lifecycle State Machine","description":" ## Formal States for Hypothesis Evolution Hypotheses aren't just "open" or "closed" - they have a rich lifecycle. This task defines the formal state machine. ### Hypothesis States 1. **Draft** - Initial creation, freely editable - No predictions locked yet - Can be deleted without penalty 2. **Active** - User is actively working on this hypothesis - Has at least one locked prediction - Cannot be deleted (only superseded or falsified) 3. **Testing** - Evidence collection in progress - Predictions locked, awaiting results - Confidence updates happening 4. **Supported** - Evidence consistent with hypothesis - High robustness score (passed tests) - May still be tested further 5. **Falsified** - Clear discriminative evidence against - Moved to Hypothesis Graveyard - Learning captured (what did we learn?) 6. **Superseded** - Replaced by a better formulation - Link to successor hypothesis - Still visible in history 7. **Dormant** - User paused work (no activity for N days) - Can be reactivated - Preserved but not shown in active list ### State Transitions - Draft → Active: First prediction locked - Active → Testing: Evidence collection starts - Testing → Supported: Predictions confirmed - Testing → Falsified: Predictions refuted - Active → Superseded: User creates refined version - Active → Dormant: No activity for 30 days - Dormant → Active: User resumes work ### Implementation - Add `state` field to HypothesisCard - State transition functions with validation - UI indicators for each state (icons, colors) - Filters in session list by state ### Depends On - an1n.1 (HypothesisCard Interface) ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:51:23.309616577-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T11:43:52.589020594-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T11:43:52.589020594-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented hypothesis lifecycle state machine with 7 states (draft, active, testing, supported, falsified, superseded, dormant), transition guards, UI configuration, and lifecycle statistics","labels":["brenner-loop","hypothesis-engine","state-machine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-se2r","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-an1n.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:51:47.763351355-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-sedg","title":"FEATURE: Undo/Redo System for Session Actions","description":" ## Research is Iterative Users will make mistakes and want to undo: - Accidentally locked a prediction too early - Recorded wrong evidence observation - Updated confidence incorrectly - Discarded a hypothesis prematurely ### Command Pattern Implementation ```typescript interface SessionCommand { id: string; type: string; timestamp: Date; // For undo execute(): Promise; undo(): Promise; // For display description: string; } class UpdateConfidenceCommand implements SessionCommand { constructor( private hypothesisId: string, private previousConfidence: number, private newConfidence: number, private evidenceId: string ) {} async execute() { await updateHypothesisConfidence(this.hypothesisId, this.newConfidence); } async undo() { await updateHypothesisConfidence(this.hypothesisId, this.previousConfidence); await markEvidenceReverted(this.evidenceId); } get description() { return `Update confidence: ${this.previousConfidence}% → ${this.newConfidence}%`; } } ``` ### Undo Stack Management ```typescript interface UndoManager { history: SessionCommand[]; undoneStack: SessionCommand[]; // For redo maxHistory: number; execute(command: SessionCommand): Promise; undo(): Promise; redo(): Promise; canUndo: boolean; canRedo: boolean; } ``` ### Undoable Actions - Confidence updates - Hypothesis state changes - Evidence recording - Prediction modifications (before lock) - Session phase transitions (with warning) ### Non-Undoable Actions (by design) - Prediction LOCK (intentionally irreversible) - Session export/share - Agent dispatch (can't un-ask an agent) ### UI Integration ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Session: Social Media Hypothesis │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ [↩ Undo] [↪ Redo] History ▾ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Recent Actions: │ │ • Updated confidence 72% → 49% [↩] │ │ • Recorded evidence: Algorithm... [↩] │ │ • Applied Level Split operator [↩] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Keyboard Shortcuts - Ctrl+Z / Cmd+Z: Undo - Ctrl+Shift+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z: Redo ### Persistence Undo history persisted with session (LocalStorage): - Survives page refresh - Cleared on session close ### File Location `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/undoManager.ts` `apps/web/src/hooks/useUndoRedo.ts` `apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/UndoRedoControls.tsx` ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Core undo/redo works for main actions - [ ] History preserved across refresh - [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work - [ ] Non-undoable actions clearly marked ### Depends On - 1v26.3 (Session Context Provider) - reew.3 (Session State Machine) ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:01.727561365-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:23:45.98107234-05:00","labels":["brenner-loop","session-ui","undo-redo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-sedg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:42.539185749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-sedg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-reew.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:44.070222339-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-sedg","title":"FEATURE: Undo/Redo System for Session Actions","description":" ## Research is Iterative Users will make mistakes and want to undo: - Accidentally locked a prediction too early - Recorded wrong evidence observation - Updated confidence incorrectly - Discarded a hypothesis prematurely ### Command Pattern Implementation ```typescript interface SessionCommand { id: string; type: string; timestamp: Date; // For undo execute(): Promise; undo(): Promise; // For display description: string; } class UpdateConfidenceCommand implements SessionCommand { constructor( private hypothesisId: string, private previousConfidence: number, private newConfidence: number, private evidenceId: string ) {} async execute() { await updateHypothesisConfidence(this.hypothesisId, this.newConfidence); } async undo() { await updateHypothesisConfidence(this.hypothesisId, this.previousConfidence); await markEvidenceReverted(this.evidenceId); } get description() { return `Update confidence: ${this.previousConfidence}% → ${this.newConfidence}%`; } } ``` ### Undo Stack Management ```typescript interface UndoManager { history: SessionCommand[]; undoneStack: SessionCommand[]; // For redo maxHistory: number; execute(command: SessionCommand): Promise; undo(): Promise; redo(): Promise; canUndo: boolean; canRedo: boolean; } ``` ### Undoable Actions - Confidence updates - Hypothesis state changes - Evidence recording - Prediction modifications (before lock) - Session phase transitions (with warning) ### Non-Undoable Actions (by design) - Prediction LOCK (intentionally irreversible) - Session export/share - Agent dispatch (can't un-ask an agent) ### UI Integration ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Session: Social Media Hypothesis │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ [↩ Undo] [↪ Redo] History ▾ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Recent Actions: │ │ • Updated confidence 72% → 49% [↩] │ │ • Recorded evidence: Algorithm... [↩] │ │ • Applied Level Split operator [↩] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Keyboard Shortcuts - Ctrl+Z / Cmd+Z: Undo - Ctrl+Shift+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z: Redo ### Persistence Undo history persisted with session (LocalStorage): - Survives page refresh - Cleared on session close ### File Location `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/undoManager.ts` `apps/web/src/hooks/useUndoRedo.ts` `apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/UndoRedoControls.tsx` ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Core undo/redo works for main actions - [ ] History preserved across refresh - [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work - [ ] Non-undoable actions clearly marked ### Depends On - 1v26.3 (Session Context Provider) - reew.3 (Session State Machine) ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:01.727561365-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:41:19.629196096-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T23:41:19.629196096-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Command Pattern undo/redo system with tests and UI controls","labels":["brenner-loop","session-ui","undo-redo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-sedg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:42.539185749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-sedg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-reew.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:44.070222339-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-slcc","title":"Task: Define State Transition Types","description":"# Task: Define State Transition Types ## Objective Define the TypeScript types for state transitions between hypothesis lifecycle states. ## Implementation Details ### File Location `apps/web/src/lib/hypothesis-transitions.ts` ### Type Definitions ```typescript import { z } from 'zod'; import { HypothesisState, HypothesisStateSchema } from './hypothesis'; export const TransitionTriggerSchema = z.enum([ 'activate', // proposed → active 'refine', // active → refined 'kill', // active → killed 'validate', // active → validated 'park', // proposed|active → dormant 'reactivate', // dormant → active ]); export type TransitionTrigger = z.infer; export const StateTransitionSchema = z.object({ hypothesisId: z.string(), fromState: HypothesisStateSchema, toState: HypothesisStateSchema, trigger: TransitionTriggerSchema, triggeredBy: z.string().optional(), // Test ID, agent name, or 'user' reason: z.string().optional(), metadata: z.record(z.unknown()).optional(), // Additional context timestamp: z.string().datetime(), }); export type StateTransition = z.infer; ``` ### Transition Matrix ```typescript /** Valid state transitions: [fromState] → [toState[]] */ export const VALID_TRANSITIONS: Record = { proposed: ['active', 'dormant'], active: ['refined', 'killed', 'validated', 'dormant'], dormant: ['active'], refined: [], // Terminal killed: [], // Terminal validated: ['refined', 'killed'], // Can be re-evaluated }; /** Which trigger causes which transition */ export const TRIGGER_TRANSITIONS: Record = { activate: { from: ['proposed', 'dormant'], to: 'active' }, refine: { from: ['active', 'validated'], to: 'refined' }, kill: { from: ['active', 'validated'], to: 'killed' }, validate: { from: ['active'], to: 'validated' }, park: { from: ['proposed', 'active'], to: 'dormant' }, reactivate: { from: ['dormant'], to: 'active' }, }; ``` ### Validation Function ```typescript export function isValidTransition( fromState: HypothesisState, toState: HypothesisState ): boolean { return VALID_TRANSITIONS[fromState]?.includes(toState) ?? false; } export function getRequiredTrigger( fromState: HypothesisState, toState: HypothesisState ): TransitionTrigger | null { for (const [trigger, config] of Object.entries(TRIGGER_TRANSITIONS)) { if (config.from.includes(fromState) && config.to === toState) { return trigger as TransitionTrigger; } } return null; } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All transition types defined with Zod - [ ] Transition matrix covers all valid paths - [ ] Validation function correctly rejects invalid transitions - [ ] Terminal states (killed, refined) cannot transition out - [ ] Clear error messages for invalid transitions ## Dependencies - Feature: Hypothesis Registry Schema (for HypothesisState type) ## Effort Estimate ~100 lines of code, ~1 hour","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:01.959129257-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:58:34.729951066-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:58:34.729951066-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in apps/web/src/lib/schemas/hypothesis-lifecycle.ts (576 lines) with 72 passing tests. Implementation exceeds spec: includes TransitionHistoryStore, convenience functions, full error handling. Uses slightly different terminology (refuted/superseded vs killed/refined) but semantically equivalent.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-slcc","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-6n4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:53.083563388-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-slcc","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-lm3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:53.265882561-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-slcc","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:40:58.36834312-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-su9","title":"Audit: deep security/reliability pass (web+CLI)","description":"Deep review of cross-cutting agent-written code (auth/proxy, Agent Mail client/contracts, artifact merge/linter, corpus fetch, installers/workflows). Identify root causes of bugs/security/reliability issues, fix with tests, and report to team via Agent Mail.","notes":"Found + fixed CLI path traversal: session write default out-file used raw threadId (artifacts/.md). Added threadId filename sanitization + warning + bun test coverage; UBS diff clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:44:52.314767269-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T10:41:00.905047294-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T10:41:00.905047294-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: security/reliability audit fixes landed (CLI threadId path traversal hardening + mail read shows message body + web tests/UX tweaks) and quality gates passing.","comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"brenner_bot-su9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Follow-on fixes from audit: fixed Method page test fragility with (Testing Library getByText ignores descendant text), improved /api/sessions/actions route test typing (avoid any), hid raw-response filenames in corpus doc cards, and fixed CLI 'mail read' to actually return message content + mark read. Quality gates: bun test brenner.test.ts + bun run test (Home/Corpus/Method page tests) + ubs --diff clean.","created_at":"2025-12-30T15:38:51Z"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-t62l","title":"Spec: Experiment result capture protocol (wrapper-run + record)","description":"# Goal Define and implement how to **capture experiment runs** so they become durable, auditable inputs to Brenner Loop sessions. This is the bridge between: - “artifact contains discriminative tests (T#)” - and “a test run produced evidence we can cite and update against” --- # v0 stance - Capture is about **what happened**, not what it means. - Execution is explicit operator action (no auto-running code from agent messages). - v0 is **wrapper-run + capture**, not a hardened sandbox. --- # Filesystem discipline (recommended) Keep a clean separation between source and outputs: - Source/workspace: the repo/folder you are studying (Agent Mail `project_key`) - Results: session-scoped directory under `artifacts/` Suggested layout: ``` / artifacts/ / results/ T1_.json ... optional generated artifacts ... ``` --- # Result schema (draft) ```ts interface ExperimentResult { // Identity result_id: string; thread_id: string; test_id: string; // Execution command: string; cwd: string; started_at: string; // ISO duration_ms: number; timeout_seconds?: number; // Outcome exit_code: number; stdout: string; stderr: string; // Optional provenance git_sha?: string; git_dirty?: boolean; files_changed?: string[]; } ``` --- # CLI commands (v0) ```bash # Wrapper-run (recommended) ./brenner.ts experiment run \ --thread-id RS-... \ --test-id T1 \ --timeout 300 \ -- python test_rrp.py --variant rrp # Manual record (when run elsewhere) ./brenner.ts experiment record \ --thread-id RS-... \ --test-id T1 \ --exit-code 0 \ --stdout-file out.txt \ --stderr-file err.txt ``` --- # Safety guidance (documented, not enforced) - Only run commands you trust. - Always use `--timeout`. - Treat stdout/stderr as potentially containing secrets. - Prefer running from a clean git state; record `git_sha` + dirty state. --- # Acceptance criteria - [ ] `ExperimentResult` contract is locked (schema + fixtures) - [ ] `experiment run` captures stdout/stderr/exit code/duration + provenance - [ ] `experiment record` exists for manual entry - [ ] Result files are session-scoped and easy to locate ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:50:52.403902581-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:50:15.656443873-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:50:15.656443873-05:00","close_reason":"Spec complete at specs/experiment_capture_protocol_v0.1.md. Implementation verified in brenner.ts with experiment run/record commands and 12 passing tests.","labels":["infrastructure","research-loop","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-t62l","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:59:49.820667903-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
